checks.finding: T-001 through T-005 affect integrity, review-gate, validation, or CI control flow and are consistently classified high with Required Workflow tdd. T-006 is a reversible release-metadata change classified medium with acceptance-first. Each rationale names the relevant harm surface, and every high-risk task plans Red-to-Green, independent review, provenance, and requirement-traceability evidence.

The round-1 finding is unresolved. T-002 Done When still names only the glob `specs/workflow-state-integrity/verification/T-002.*.log` for both Red and Green evidence instead of two concrete evidence files.
checks.failure_mode: The implementer and check-contract cannot deterministically bind the required tdd `red_evidence` and `green_evidence` fields to distinct, existing artifacts; a wildcard may match zero, one, or unrelated files.
checks.recommendation: Name exact separate paths, for example `specs/workflow-state-integrity/verification/T-002.red.log` and `specs/workflow-state-integrity/verification/T-002.green.log`, and state which command produces each.

checks.finding: The round-1 finding is unresolved. T-001, T-003, and T-005 list several Planned Files as bare basenames after one fully qualified path, including `impl-review-precheck.ps1`, `task-review-precheck.sh`, `task-review-precheck.ps1`, `check-workflow-state.ps1`, `tests/run-all.sh`, and `tests/run-all.ps1`.
checks.failure_mode: Bare basenames are not exact repository-relative paths and can resolve to the wrong directory or leave the implementation scope/changed-file allowlist ambiguous, especially where similarly named shell and PowerShell adapters exist.
checks.recommendation: Replace every bare basename with its complete repository-relative path; do not rely on the preceding list item to imply a directory.

checks.finding: T-005 is the task that wires global validation into quality-gate and scoped validation into Impl/Task prechecks, but its Requirements field maps only AC-012. AC-010 explicitly requires inspection and execution of those full quality-gate and downstream precheck paths. AC-010 is instead assigned only to T-001, whose scope is the predecessor transition hash/path repair and whose Out of Scope excludes global repository-state policy.
checks.failure_mode: The task implementing AC-010 can be completed and traced without claiming AC-010, while the task claiming AC-010 can pass without implementing the required workflow-state integration. This breaks semantic acceptance-to-task traceability despite nominal AC coverage.
checks.recommendation: Add AC-010 to T-005 and retain AC-012 for regression-suite coverage. Narrow T-001's AC mapping to the transition-repair evidence it actually owns, or explicitly state the distinct AC-010 portion it verifies.

checks.finding: Overlapping files are ordered safely: T-001 precedes T-003 and therefore T-005 transitively; T-004 and T-005 precede T-006, which also edits repository validation metadata. T-003 depends on the registry from T-002, and integration tasks depend on the standalone validator.

Each task provides a scoped rollback unit and an inspectable post-rollback validation or isolated-fixture exercise. Registry/history rollback avoids mutating historical artifacts, validator adapters roll back together, integrations preserve the standalone validator, and release metadata rolls back atomically.

T-001 preserves the required diagnostic chain: it names the observed post-transition rejection, requires reproduction of the original failure, diagnoses canonical hash/path mismatch, narrows the status-neutral hash and path-canonicalization fix, requires regression rejection cases, and records passing post-fix evidence. The separate evidence-path precision issue is owned by T002-VERIFICATION-PATH.
